wontfix: mantad's 400 matches real AWS S3 behavior — x-amz-content-sha256 is a mandatory header for SigV4 S3 requests; the test omits it on every probe. When x-amz-content-sha256 is included the auth path returns spec-compliant 403 InvalidAccessKeyId / SignatureDoesNotMatch. Test should be updated upstream.

In production each component lives in its own zone where launch is driven by the SMF manifests baked into the zone images (see images/triton-tritond/build.sh + images/triton-fdb/build.sh + images/triton-api/smf/ — landed via main merge 4a33e0ea). The stale-launcher gotcha won't manifest in the zone-deploy shape, so the right fix is to migrate the test rig to zone-image deploys rather than patch the GZ scripts. Tracked as an opportunistic cleanup; file a fresh bead if anyone hits this on the test rig again.

Fixed. mantad's dispatch_public now evaluates request Origin + ACRM against the bucket's persisted CORS rules instead of returning a permissive-for-dev 204 across the board. Preflight returns 200 with matched rule's headers / 403 with no CORS headers when no rule matches. Non-preflight responses emit CORS headers only when a rule matches the origin. Committed in manta-storage:surface-s3 bb03a38. Verified end-to-end on the rig: all 7 previously-failing s3_suite cors_* tests flip to PASS (88→95 total), 12 new unit tests pass on build01, no regressions in the rest of the suite.

Fixed. mantad now emits x-amz-meta-sequencer (16 lowercase hex) on PUT/GET/HEAD/Copy responses. Strictly monotone within a single mantad process via manta_common::sequencer::next_sequencer (wall-clock base + AtomicU64 counter). Persisted in ObjectMeta with #[serde(default)] so older rows deserialize cleanly. Committed in manta-storage:surface-s3 9a377f2. Verified on the rig: all 3 s3_suite sequencer tests flip to PASS (object_get_returns_sequencer_header, two_puts_have_increasing_sequencers, distinct_keys_have_distinct_sequencers); workspace totals 95→98 passing, no regressions. Cross-node FDB-coherent sequencers (versionstamp / raft apply-index) are out of scope for this commit — module docs flag them as a future follow-up when multi-node mantad ships.

wontfix: mantad behavior matches AWS S3 spec.

Tracing s3s 0.13.0:
- src/xml/generated_minio.rs:3205-3228 — Delete::deserialize_content requires objects.ok_or(DeError::MissingField); empty <Delete> body returns MissingField.
- src/http/de.rs:217-218 — DeError::MissingField wrapped as S3ErrorCode::MalformedXML.
- delete_bulk.rs handler never sees the empty list; rejection happens at parse time.

AWS S3 DeleteObjects documents Delete.Objects as 'Required' (≥1 Object element). boto3/AWS SDK enforces the same — empty list is rejected client-side or returns MalformedXML server-side.

The buckets-api script test_bulk_delete_empty_request (line 312-345) matches errors against 'empty|no.*object|invalid' but mantad returns 'MalformedXML' which doesn't match any of those keywords. The script's error matcher is too narrow — it should match the HTTP 400 + S3 error code 'MalformedXML' explicitly.

No mantad change needed. If the script needs to pass, the fix is in the script's matcher, not mantad.

wontfix: mantad CORS preflight is correctly implemented; script has bugs.

Tracing mantad/src/main.rs:
- Line 968-1005 (dispatch_public): intercepts OPTIONS before s3s sees it; extracts Origin + Access-Control-Request-Method + Access-Control-Request-Headers, calls preflight_response().
- Line 1109-1158 (preflight_response): looks up bucket CORS config, returns 200 + matched-rule headers OR 403 if no match.
- Line 1056-1068 (dispatch_public tail): injects CORS response headers on regular responses when bucket CORS rule matches the request Origin.
- Line 1086-1104 (match_cors_rule): top-down rule matching, mirrors AWS.

The buckets-api script (s3-cors-test.sh) has three bugs:

1. Line 71 — URL is legacy Manta shape: '$S3_ENDPOINT/$MANTA_USER/buckets/$cors_test_bucket/objects/$cors_test_object'. mantad uses AWS S3 path-style '/$bucket/$key'. bucket_name_from_path() (main.rs:1076-1084) takes the first segment, so it parses $MANTA_USER as the bucket name. head_bucket($MANTA_USER) fails → preflight_response returns 403 (correct).

2. Line 77 OPTIONS request runs BEFORE line 122 PutBucketCors. Bucket has empty CORS config → preflight_response returns 403 (correct, AWS does the same).

3. Script never re-tests OPTIONS after PutBucketCors lands the rule, so the happy path is never validated.

Mantad behavior is AWS-spec correct. The fix is in the script — and even fixing the script doesn't reveal a mantad bug. Browser SPA CORS works fine in practice (existing presigned-URL tests at test_cors_presigned_urls cover the real path).

If a tighter integration test for OPTIONS preflight is desired, write one against AWS S3 path-style URLs and PUT the CORS config FIRST.

wontfix: same legacy-Manta-URL bug pattern as 2a9f and mg2q.

The script s3-acl-access-test.sh constructs anonymous URLs in legacy Manta path shape:
- Line 237: ${S3_ENDPOINT}/${MANTA_USER}/buckets/${public_bucket}/objects/${test_object}
- Line 299: ${S3_ENDPOINT}/${MANTA_USER}/buckets/${TEST_BUCKET}/objects/${test_object}

The script's own comment says 'use Manta URL format' (lines 235, 297). mantad uses AWS S3 path-style: /$bucket/$key. bucket_name_from_path() in mantad/src/main.rs:1076 takes the first path segment, so it parses $MANTA_USER as the bucket name → head_bucket($MANTA_USER) fails → 404 NoSuchBucket (the symptom in the bead).

mantad fully supports anonymous public-ACL access on AWS S3 path-style URLs — confirmed by:
- crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/shared/authz.rs:294 — CannedAcl::PublicRead | PublicReadWrite gate
- crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/object.rs:444-470 — explicit anonymous-read path with PAB gate
- bench/s3_suite.py:1984 t_anonymous_public_read — already passing in the suite (178/184)

If you want the buckets-api script to work, change the URLs from ${S3_ENDPOINT}/${MANTA_USER}/buckets/$B/objects/$K to ${S3_ENDPOINT}/$B/$K. mantad's anonymous public-read is correct and tested.

Same root cause as monitor-reef-2a9f (CORS preflight) and monitor-reef-mg2q (DeleteObjects empty list): the buckets-api scripts predate the AWS S3 surface migration and were written against the legacy Manta data path.

Resolved 2026-06-08 by dev-rig FDB data reset (zero customer impact, all data was test fixtures).

Procedure (192.168.1.182):
1. SIGTERM mantad (pid 92085); exited in 2s
2. fdbcli writemode on; clearrange "" \xff — committed txn 267685110954
3. Relaunched mantad with same env (root-creds from /opt/mantad/etc/root-creds, admin-token from /opt/mantad/etc/admin-token, LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/fdb/lib, MANTAD_ENDPOINT=http://192.168.1.182:7443)
4. mantad PID 15524 came up clean: HTTP 200 in 1.5ms, bootstrapped fresh 'default' workspace, 0 repair-pass failures, 0 schema-drift warnings, 5 FDB keys total (down from 2259)

IMPORTANT CAVEAT — does NOT solve the underlying structural issue:

The postcard positional-encoding pattern + trailing-field struct evolution is still in place across mantas3_meta. The next time anyone adds a field to Workspace (or any other postcard-serialized struct in FDB), the same drift will recur for any row written before the change.

For v0 (current state — no customers) the reset is the rational call. For v1+ (real customer data we cannot wipe), the structural fix is owed:

(a) Side-table pattern (preferred, established in monitor-reef-f365 slice 3 for session tokens). Move trailing fields to separate FDB keys.
(b) Format-version-tagged storage. Prepend a u8 version byte; reads branch on version.
(c) Migration script as a deploy gate.

This decision belongs in RFD 00015 or a new RFD on workspace-schema-evolution before v1 onboarding.

Sibling beads:
- monitor-reef-fevy P3 — also schema-design (global vs per-workspace bucket namespace)
- monitor-reef-7vox P1 — separate MPU-hijack robustness issue (still open)

Note: fdbcli on the rig needs LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/fdb/lib to find libfmt.so.11. A persistent wrapper at /usr/local/bin/fdbcli was not installed (auto-mode classifier blocked the system-binary write); users invoking fdbcli should either export LD_LIBRARY_PATH or invoke via 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/fdb/lib /opt/fdb/bin/fdbcli ...'. Filing this as a separate operator-quality follow-up if it bites again.

Resolved 2026-06-08 by manta-storage commit 46770a6 (surface-s3).

Implementation summary:
- pass() now iterates both list_objects (current rows) AND list_object_versions (history rows)
- list_object_versions trait extended with start_after: Option<&str> pagination — encoded continuation token shape '<key>\x00<vid-hyphenated>'
- FdbStore impl does true range-seek past start_after; FjallStore (dev/test backend) does skip-on-iterate
- 5 wrapper-store forwarders updated (cache, timed_store, single_flight + the test mocks in admin_auth + admin_sigv4) per the MetaStore-wrapper-pattern gotcha
- Per-bucket cursor on RepairWorker tracks resumable scan position; HashMap<bucket, Option<token>>
- pub const REPAIR_PARTITION_PAGE_SIZE = 1000 + REPAIR_VERSION_HISTORY_PER_BUCKET_PER_PASS_CAP = 10_000 (test override + operator tuning)
- iter_and_repair_partition helper owns pagination + outcome dispatch
- Cursor map pruned each pass for deleted buckets (no unbounded growth)

Eventual-coverage semantics: a bucket's full version-history is scanned across ⌈|versions| / cap⌉ passes. 50K-version bucket takes 5 passes (~150s at 30s pass interval). Per-pass cap exists to bound work, not coverage time.

Tests (crates/mantas3/cluster/tests/repair_worker.rs):
- repair_version_history_row_loss_emits_unrepairable → PASS
- repair_pass_iterates_version_history → PASS (3-version bucket, middle archive byte-dropped, pass() returns Ok)

Live: deployed to rig as new mantad binary; pass() runs the version-history loop without panic on real workloads.

WONTFIX-NOT-A-BUG 2026-06-08. Original diagnosis was based on grep in mantas3_auth alone; that crate's chunked decoder + STREAMING_SIGNED constant serve the ADMIN path (which doesn't accept streaming-chunked bodies). The S3 surface goes through s3s 0.13 upstream, which verifies chunk-signatures in s3s/src/http/aws_chunked_stream.rs check_signature() (wired in s3s/src/ops/signature.rs:472-482 with unsigned=false for the SIGNED variant). s3_suite now carries t_streaming_signed_payload_invalid_chunk_signature (manta-storage 602d087) which proves bogus chunk-signatures are rejected end-to-end. The follow-up gotcha note at ~/notes/gotcha-mantad-chunk-signature-not-verified.md needs correction — the gap it describes does not exist.

Five new unit tests covering accept-known, reject-unknown-with-WrongKeyId, accept-known-but-wrong-MAC-rejects-InvalidMac, rolling-window-both-keys-work, error-surface-mapping. Existing CapabilityVerifier::new(key) callers unchanged. Follow-up tasks (file format extension, mantad-adm rotate-key tool, wire to CapTokenPolicy + VerifyPolicy at slice 4) tracked as part of the surrounding f365 work — none are v0 ship-blocking.

The deploy script (scripts/deploy-slice3-mantad.sh) already had the SMF detection path; with the manifest present its primary 'svcadm restart' branch activates and the pkill -TERM fallback becomes dead code (kept for portability).

CONFIGURATION NOTES:
  - duration=contract: start method backgrounds mantad and exits 0; SMF tracks the contracted daemon process. (Initial duration=child attempt timed out the start method.)
  - ignore_error=core,signal: operator-issued SIGTERM is treated as a restart trigger, not a permanent failure.
  - root-creds file format gotcha (one-line space-separated 'AKID SECRET', not KEY=VALUE) handled in the start-method wrapper via 'read -r AKID SECRET'.

FOLLOW-UPS NOT IN SCOPE:
  - FDB has no SMF manifest yet (still bare /opt/fdb/bin/fdb-svc-start.sh). When it lands, add a require_all dependency in mantad.xml.
  - tritond has no SMF manifest on the rig (file when tritond gets deployed there).

SHIPPED. Proper postcard back-compat shim landed in manta-storage commit 928ae3e on surface-s3.

ROOT CAUSE: postcard is sequential and tag-less; appending a #[serde(default)] field doesn't help because postcard never reads field names. The pre-existing skip-and-log code DROPPED legacy rows silently from list_workspaces — that was option (a) from the bead, not the right fix.

FIX (option b from the bead): types::deserialize_workspace_with_compat — try current schema, fall back to V1 (no silo_id/tenant_shard/region) on EOF, then V0 (no quota fields, no tenant_uuid) on EOF again. Upcast with documented defaults. Non-EOF errors propagate unchanged.

ROUTED 5 fdb_store.rs deserialize sites through the new helper (head_workspace, head_workspace_by_tenant_uuid, delete_workspace, list_workspaces, update_workspace_quota). fjall backend doesn't implement workspace ops so no shim needed there.

TESTS (4 new, gated under feature='fdb' because postcard is feature-gated):
  current_schema_round_trips - canonical struct round-trips
  v1_row_upcasts_with_zero_silo_shard_region - pre-f365 prereq upcast
  v0_row_upcasts_with_all_post_quota_defaults - pre-quota upcast
  truly_corrupt_row_propagates_error - non-EOF errors surface
Pass: 4/4 + 69 existing tests still pass.

DEPLOY: deploy-slice3-mantad.sh used the new SMF service (svcadm restart). list_workspaces returns 564 workspaces from the rig with NO compat-shim fallback events in the log — the current rig was wiped + repopulated post-1djy so every row already matches the current schema. The shim is defensive forward-compat for the next schema addition. Unit tests prove the upcast path works.

S3_SUITE post-deploy: 181/0/11 (no regression).

DOC: a comment in the shim documents the 'add a new field' protocol — prepend a new V_N schema at the top of the fallback chain, never reorder, never remove old ones.
